content.full_text:
MARY OSHER
& ASSOCIATES

JUL 2 1991

FLUOR RANGI/
POLITCAL CONSULTANTS
660 FIFTH STREET, SUITE 203
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94107
(415) 777-2340

July 26, 1991


California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for Confirmation in Writing

Dear Sir/Madam:

I am currently in the process of organizing a campaign committee for
San Francisco Supervisor Jim Gonzalez for his November 1992 re-election bid.

From several conversations which I have had over the past weeks with a couple
of consultants in your office, I have been informed of the following committee
guidelines:

1.     Supervisor Gonzalez may open a committee and begin receiving
       funds for his November 1992 campaign.

2.     Supervisor Gonzalez may keep his officeholder committee
       (ID#890550) in use while collecting funds for his 1992 campaign.
       Expenditures from this committee must be non-campaign related,
       as has been the procedure.

3.     Supervisor Gonzalez may use officeholder committee funds up
       until 90 days prior to the November 1992 election. At that time, all
       expenses are considered by the PPPC to be campaign related, and
       therefore, must be taken from the campaign committee.

4.     Supervisor Gonzalez should check with the San Francisco
       City Attorney's office regarding the city ordinance which limits
       campaign contributions to $500 per person, business, labor union, etc.

I would greatly appreciate your office writing to me at my address below indicating
that the above guidelines are correct.

page 1 of 2

California Fair Political Practices Commission
July 26, 1991
page 2 of 2


Thank you in advance for your assistance.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Ron Wong
Treasurer
Supervisor Jim Gonzalez

address:  650 5th Street, Suite 203
          San Francisco, CA 94107-1521
phone:    415/777-2340

cc:  Supervisor Jim Gonzalez
     Randy Riddle, S.F. City Attorney's Office

July 25, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
Attn: Mrs. Alice Hughes
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95812

Dear Mrs. Hughes:

Thank you for your help today. I would like you to confirm in writing
the answer you gave me verbally today so that I may have an official
statement for my records:

My question was based on the following facts:

     (1) I intend to be a candidate for the office of City Councilman
of Foster City, California. The election is scheduled for November
5, 1991.

     (2) I have previously been elected to the same office twice (May,
1977 and again in April, 1980) and I served until the end of my
second term in April, 1984. I have not served on the City Council
since April, 1984.

My question is as follows: Can I use the word "re-elect" in the name of
my committee and on my political signs and advertising materials?
Specifically, I would like to name my committee the "Committee to Re-
Elect David R. Kruss", and would like to use the phrase "Re-Elect David
Kruss" on my signs and advertising materials.

You have researched my question and have indicated to me verbally that
the use of the word "Re-elect" would be permitted under the
circumstances indicated above, and that I could use such designation
without being misleading or in violation of the FPPC Act.

Please confirm in writing that answer you gave me is correct under the
circumstances stated above.

Sincerely,

David R. Kruss
312 Menhaden Court
Foster City, CA 94404-1933
Home # (415) 574-8366
Bus # (415) 573-7202
